messages.content:
How is Accenture addressing the emerging market for quantum computing technology?


by building our own quantum computers that will be hosted in Accenture offices


by adopting a Quantum-as-a-Service approach using existing quantum computers


by advising our clients to replace all their classical computers with quantum machines


by taking a "wait and see" approach until quantum computers are developed further
by adopting a Quantum-as-a-Service approach using existing quantum computers

Which term refers to the strong information correlation between qubits?


coherence


quantum annealing


entanglement


linear combination
entanglement

What is an example of using quantum computing for sustainable practices?


storing large computing servers in high-altitude conditions


mining cryptocurrency using multiple connected computers


installing edge computing devices in common public places


improving the efficiency of batteries on electric cars
improving the efficiency of batteries on electric cars

What is a benefit of interference in quantum computing?


It disrupts repeatable processes and allows for automation.


It allows for encryption of data within large databases.


It reinforces certain results and helps identify good paths.


It helps to untangle qubits so that they reveal better paths.
It reinforces certain results and helps identify good paths.

Why is quantum computing useful for optimization problems?


It can perform operations on a combination of all possible solutions.


It evaluates solutions one by one in a sequential manner.


It uses human intuition to only explore certain potential solutions.


It divides the intractable complexity into bits to calculate simple solutions.
It can perform operations on a combination of all possible solutions.

Why might businesses be interested in using quantum computers?


to process extremely large data sets


to create graphics for immersive environments


to run mobile apps more quickly


to solve intractable problems
to solve intractable problems.

What is the purpose of "post-quantum" cryptography?


to eliminate all encryption methods programmed on classical computers


to require users to own quantum computers in order to send data securely


to use quantum computers to create more secure encryption methods


to create encryption methods that quantum computers cannot crack
to create encryption methods that quantum computers cannot crack.

What is the term for breaking a larger number apart into smaller numbers that can be multiplied together to get a specific result?


entanglement


superposition


factorization


interference
factorization

Why is quantum computing potentially a better fit for weather forecasting than classical computers?


It can be easily installed at locations around the globe.


It can function efficiently when stored at high temperatures.


It can store extensive data for better pattern recognition.


It can perform advanced simulations more efficiently.
It can perform advanced simulations more efficiently.

How could quantum computing benefit the Financial Services industry?


by guaranteeing double returns for fund managers


by determining future value of crypto-currencies


by enabling complex modeling to optimize portfolios


by predicting market activities months in advance
by enabling complex modeling to optimize portfolios.

What is a current concern regarding the advancement of quantum computing?


Existing cryptography may be easily cracked.


Algorithmic trading may cause stock market instability.


Computers will replace humans in all decision-making tasks.


Steering qubits towards desired states will introduce bias.
Existing cryptography may be easily cracked.

Which part of the drug discovery life cycle can quantum computing impact the most?


molecular simulation


laboratory testing


clinical trials


regulatory approval
molecular simulation

What is meant by "applied quantum computing?"


installing quantum computers directly in company offices


processing simple mathematical functions more quickly


using quantum computers to solve real business problems


building more advanced quantum computing hardware
using quantum computers to solve real business problems.

How is a qubit in quantum computing different from a regular bit in classical computing?


A qubit stores information as graphical images.


A qubit can represent an indeterminate state.


A qubit can represent a value as 0, 1, or 2.


A qubit takes up less memory space than a regular bit.
A qubit can represent an indeterminate state.

Which statement describes the current availability of quantum computers?


They are widely available and affordable in consumer retail stores.


They are owned by a few companies, but available to many.


They will be developed for home installation within the next decade.


They are currently only available on certain mobile devices.
They are owned by a few companies, but available to many.

What is the difference between Infrastructure as a Service (IaaS) and Software as a Service (SaaS)?


IaaS is a form of Cloud computing that provides hardware and software tools over the internet. SaaS is a form of Cloud computing that provides virtualized computing resources over the internet.


IaaS is Cloud computing that provides virtualized computing resources over the internet. SaaS is a form of Cloud computing that provides hosted applications over the internet.


IaaS is a form of Cloud computing that provides virtualized computing resources over the internet. SaaS is a form of Cloud computing that provides hardware and software tools over the internet.


IaaS is Cloud computing that provides hardware and software tools over the internet. SaaS is Cloud computing that provides database solutions over the internet.
IaaS is Cloud computing that provides virtualized computing resources over the internet. SaaS is a form of Cloud computing that provides hosted applications over the internet.

Accenture is meeting with a well-known, global financial institution that specializes in private and commercial banking to discuss the migration of its sizable application ecosystem to the Cloud. The client has quickly become overwhelmed with the available choices, complexity, and security.

Which tool helps the client self-assess their Cloud landscape to determine the best solution?


DiPA (Digital People Advisor)


Work Management System (WMS)


myConcerto


myNav
myNav

In the future, where will an increasing majority of data come from?


corporate office headquarters


centralized data warehouses


individual users and devices


physical memory storage drives
individual users and devices

The Ocean Medallion is a wearable device that connects virtually to various devices on board a cruise ship. When a guest is wearing the medallion, they can order a drink with their smartphone or open their cabin door automatically by standing in front of it.

Which emerging technology does Cloud combine with to enable this medallion's features?


Internet of Things (IoT)


Extended Reality


Data Visualization​


DevOps
Internet of Things (IoT)

What would be an ideal scenario for using edge computing solutions?


a hospital department that performs high-risk, time-sensitive surgeries


a regional sales report uploaded to a central server once a week


a farming business whose equipment is older and lacks digital devices


an office where workers are directly connected to the company network
a hospital department that performs high-risk, time-sensitive surgeries.

A healthcare client approaches Accenture for help in defining their Cloud migration strategy. During the initial discussions, the client explains that there are very specific regulatory and other compliance requirements within their industry.

Which Cloud deployment model would give this client complete control over their data and security?


Private Cloud


Multi-Cloud


Public Cloud


Open Cloud Model
Private Cloud

An Infrastructure as a Service (IaaS) provider shares its computing resources between a number of different clients, each of whom has a separate dedicated instance to host their own operating systems and applications.

Which Cloud computing concept is illustrated here?


elasticity


resiliency


multi-regional support


multitenancy
multitenancy

What describes the relationship between edge computing and Cloud computing?


Edge computing will eventually overtake and replace Cloud computing.


Cloud computing is a continuum of which edge is a key component.


Edge computing sits at the center of the network while Cloud sits at the periphery.


Cloud computing originally began as an extension of edge computing.
Cloud computing is a continuum of which edge is a key component.

A Software Developer wants to add a new feature to an existing application operating in the Cloud, but only wants to pay for the computing time the code actually uses when it is called.

Which term describes this feature of computing?


serverless computing


elasticity


multitenancy


pooled resources
serverless computing

What is a characteristic of Cloud computing?


It reserves a certain portion of its computing resources to individual clients.


It limits access to each application to one user at a time in order to avoid conflicts.


It allows multiple customers to share applications while retaining data privacy.


It provides fixed computing resources based on the terms of the client contract.
It allows multiple customers to share applications while retaining data privacy.

How does a Cloud-first strategy approach a client's migration to the Cloud?


by focusing on a single discipline based on the client's greatest area of need


by bringing multiple services together to serve the client's business needs


by removing outside ecosystem partners from the Cloud transition process


by enabling the client to manage their Cloud transformation on their own
by bringing multiple services together to serve the client's business needs.

Which capability of myNav helps clients with reskilling their employees?


discovery and assessment


migrations and operations


design and architecture


talent transformation
talent transformation

An electronics company wants to use a type of Cloud computing architecture that would enable them to use Microsoft Azure as a platform, while storing and retrieving data in AWS.

Which type of Cloud deployment would meet the company’s business needs?


Private Cloud


Public Cloud


Hybrid Cloud


Multi-Cloud
Multi-Cloud

A chain of dry-cleaning outlets wants to improve its operations by using data from devices at individual locations to make real-time adjustments to service delivery.

Which technology would the business combine with its current Cloud operations to make this possible?


Edge Computing


Public Cloud


Data Visualization


Blockchain
Edge Computing

What distinguishes Accenture’s Cloud capabilities from our competitors?


allowing clients to choose the memory, location, and speed of the Cloud services that they want to use


providing end-to-end solutioning that includes technology, innovation, and industry specialization


offering mobile accessibility for corporate data via smartphones and other devices


enabling clients to host Cloud services using their own on-premise infrastructure
providing end-to-end solutioning that includes technology, innovation, and industry specialization.

What is an example of an Intelligent Automation solution that makes use of Artificial Intelligence?


filling out forms with basic contact information


signing-in to various desktop applications


copying text from a web browser


recognizing and interpreting natural language
recognizing and interpreting natural language.

Analysts at a security firm were tasked with monitoring a series of live cameras to detect possible threats, but this led to user error and missing potential red flags. The firm builds a system that analyzes data from the cameras to identify possible threats and flag them for human attention.

Which type of automation does this example describe?


Intelligent Automation


Robotic Process Automation


Mechanization


Personal Automation
Intelligent Automation.

What is the simplest form of automation?


Intelligent Automation


Robotics


Mechanization


Robotic Process Automation
Mechanization.

What key value does Accenture’s myConcerto bring to clients looking for automated solutions?


myConcerto provides clients with a suite of ready-to-use productivity dashboards.


myConcerto uses slide presentations to demonstrate the capabilities of Accenture’s automated solutions.


myConcerto lets clients work side-by-side with Accenture to tailor and visualize their solutions.


myConcerto functions an out-of-the-box platform solution that Accenture can hand off to clients.
myConcerto lets clients work side-by-side with Accenture to tailor and visualize their solutions.

In the context of automation strategy, what is a Center of Excellence?


a team that establishes best practices for automation efforts within an organization


a team composed of the organization’s most senior Artificial Intelligence programmers


a central server where all of an organization’s automation software is hosted


a community that includes all those within an organization who have created automation programs
a team that establishes best practices for automation efforts within an organization.

What is the first step to approaching automation?


installing applications used to develop automation


implementing new business processes


speeding up current operating procedures


aligning automation with business strategy
aligning automation with business strategy.

What is a best practice when approaching an automation effort?


prioritizing cost reduction above all other goals


eliminating the need for a technical support team


acquiring new resources before starting automation work


improving the current process before automating it
improving the current process before automating it.

What describes Accenture's approach to automation?


machine-centered


human-centered


intelligence-centered


cloud-centered
human-centered

A regional bank implemented an automated solution to streamline their operations for receiving and processing checks. They would like to further enhance the solution to recognize signs of potential fraud and alert their security teams accordingly.

Which technology could be combined with the current solution to do this?


Mechanization


Robotic Process Automation


Machine Learning


Sentiment Analysis
Machine Learning.

What is the main difference between an automated Digital Worker and a traditional automated Bot?


A Digital Worker focuses on a single specific task.


A Bot requires Artificial Intelligence capabilities to function.


A Bot is a more advanced version of a Digital Worker.


A Digital Worker can manage multi-faceted situations.
A Digital Worker can manage multi-faceted situations.

A software team is setting an automation strategy and begins by identifying business processes that could benefit from automation.

What should be the team’s next step after this?


building and testing the automated solutions


defining the processes in clear and unambiguous language


planning the rollout of the automated solutions


creating a Center of Excellence for automation
defining the processes in clear and unambiguous language.

An e-commerce platform is looking for a more efficient way to categorize survey results and social media interactions to learn what makes their customers happy or frustrated.

Which technique will be helpful for them in this case?


Predictive Analysis


Sentiment Analysis


Intrusive Automation


Personal Automation
Sentiment Analysis.

How do Design Tools build robots for a Robotic Process Automation (RPA) application?


through scripts written in a low-level programming language such as Assembly or C


through scripts written in a high-level programming language such as Java or Python


through instructions written in plain text that are parsed into jobs by the design tool


through workflows designed using drag-and-drop interfaces and minimal code instructions
through workflows designed using drag-and-drop interfaces and minimal code instructions.

A Project Manager sets up a recurring invite for meetings using a web-based calendar app on a mobile device.

What type of automation does this exemplify?


Cognitive Automation


Hyperautomation


Intelligent Automation


Personal Automation
Personal Automation.

How can the extensibility of a platform benefit a business?


by expanding the number of users a platform can support


by allowing customers to log in to the platform through an internal system


by having clear requirements that will not change throughout the platform’s lifespan


by quickly and easily adding features as the platform grows
by quickly and easily adding features as the platform grows.

Which describes a platform?


a framework that provides foundational working system for software development


a ready-to-use software package that performs a specific business function


an organized collection of data stored electronically on a local computer system or cloud


a piece of technology that connects people and other technologies together
a framework that provides foundational working system for software development.

What type of platform is SAP?


Operative system platform


Social platform


Computing platform


Enterprise platform
Enterprise platform.

A company is considering several Software as a Service (SaaS) platform providers to manage their talent lifecycle of hiring, onboarding, ongoing career development, and payroll.

Where should the company primarily focus when evaluating different SaaS providers?


on the provider’s underlying infrastructure


on the geographic location of the data centers


on the capacity of the provider’s data storage


on the features of the software itself
on the features of the software itself.
